record.field: Kimai is the leading open-source, multi-user time-tracking application for freelancers, agencies and organizations.

Kimai is the leading open-source, multi-user time-tracking application for freelancers, agencies and organizations.

Built on PHP and the Symfony framework, Kimai lets teams record working hours, manage projects and customers, and turn tracked time into reports and invoices. It scales from a single freelancer to large companies with role-based permissions, team management, and per-user, per-customer or per-project rates, all while keeping data on your own infrastructure.

Key features
  • Multi-user time tracking with multi-timer and punch-in/punch-out modes
  • Invoicing, data exports and time/money budgets per project or customer
  • Advanced reporting, search, filtering and tagging
  • Enterprise authentication: SAML, LDAP, OIDC and 2FA via TOTP
  • Role-based permissions, teams and customer/project-specific rates
  • JSON API, plugin marketplace and 30+ interface translations
Best for

Freelancers, consultancies, agencies and companies that bill by the hour and need self-hosted, privacy-friendly time tracking with built-in invoicing and reporting.

services: app: image: kimai/kimai2:apache-2.31.0 restart: unless-stopped environment: APP_ENV: prod DATABASE_URL: mysql://kimai:${KI_MYSQL_PASSWORD:-kimai}@db/kimai TRUSTED_PROXIES: localhost APP_SECRET: {{ ki_app_secret }} MAILER_FROM: ${KI_MAILER_FROM:-admin@kimai.local} MAILER_URL: ${KI_MAILER_URL:-null://null} ADMINMAIL: ${KI_ADMINMAIL:-admin@kimai.local} ADMINPASS: {{ ki_adminpass }} volumes: - ./kimai_data:/opt/kimai/var depends_on: db: condition: service_healthy container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:8001' networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network db: image: mariadb:10.11 restart: unless-stopped environment: - MYSQL_DATABASE=kimai - MYSQL_USER=kimai - MYSQL_PASSWORD={{ ki_mysql_password }} - MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD={{ ki_mysql_root_password }} volumes: - ./mysql_data:/var/lib/mysql command: - --character-set-server=utf8mb4 - --collation-server=utf8mb4_unicode_ci - --innodb-buffer-pool-size=256M - --innodb-flush-log-at-trx-commit=2 healthcheck: test: - CMD - mysqladmin - ping - -h - localhost - -u - kimai - -p{{ ki_mysql_password }} interval: 10s timeout: 5s retries: 5 start_period: 30s container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_db' networks: - internal networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Dump de la base de APLICACIÓN. Clave: usa MYSQL_PWD (env), NUNCA `-p` — con `-p` y # password vacío (apps con MYSQL_RANDOM_ROOT_PASSWORD) mysql espera un prompt por stdin # y CUELGA. Prefiere el usuario de app (MYSQL_USER/PASSWORD/DATABASE); cae a root solo si # no hay usuario de app (excluye schemas de sistema, que romperían la auth al restaurar). docker compose exec -T db sh -c ' # MariaDB 11+ renombró mysqldump→mariadb-dump y mysql→mariadb: detectamos el binario. DUMP=$(command -v mysqldump || command -v mariadb-dump); CLI=$(command -v mysql || command -v mariadb) if [ -n "${MYSQL_USER:-}" ] && [ -n "${MYSQL_PASSWORD:-}" ] && [ -n "${MYSQL_DATABASE:-}" ]; then export MYSQL_PWD="$MYSQL_PASSWORD" exec "$DUMP" -u"$MYSQL_USER" --single-transaction --databases "$MYSQL_DATABASE" fi export MYSQL_PWD="${MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD:-}" DBS=$("$CLI" -uroot -N -e "SHOW DATABASES" | grep -Ev "^(mysql|sys|information_schema|performance_schema)$") [ -z "$DBS" ] && { echo "REVISAR: sin bases de aplicacion para respaldar" >&2; exit 0; } exec "$DUMP" -uroot --single-transaction --databases $DBS ' > "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.sql" echo "mysqldump OK ($(wc -c < "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.sql") bytes)"

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" docker compose up -d db # Readiness + restore en UN exec (credenciales DENTRO del contenedor, con MYSQL_PWD → # jamás cuelga esperando un prompt de password). Prefiere el usuario de app; cae a root. docker compose exec -T db sh -c ' CLI=$(command -v mysql || command -v mariadb) if [ -n "${MYSQL_USER:-}" ] && [ -n "${MYSQL_PASSWORD:-}" ]; then U="$MYSQL_USER"; export MYSQL_PWD="$MYSQL_PASSWORD"; else U=root; export MYSQL_PWD="${MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD:-}"; fi for i in $(seq 1 60); do "$CLI" -u"$U" -e "SELECT 1" >/dev/null 2>&1 && break; sleep 2; done exec "$CLI" -u"$U" ' < "$RESTORE_DATA_DIR/db.sql" echo "restore MySQL OK"
